metrics.narrative: Cesar articulated operational pain around late-payment penalties, manual procurement processes, and lack of supplier performance visibility on the 24 June discovery call, but provided no financial quantification (late-fee costs, manual-touch hours, maverick-spend percentage, or projected ROI). He stated 'We're getting dinged on late payments and our buyers are drowning in manual PO creation' and 'We have zero visibility into which suppliers are performing and which are costing us money.' Without quantified metrics, EB/CFO approval is at risk. The 30 June discovery call was positioned to anchor metrics but Next Step does not confirm whether financial impact was quantified. Quantifying the business case is the immediate next move.

champion.narrative: Cesar Cherlin (Estimating & Procurement Manager) is the emerging champion. He initiated contact, articulated pain, asked technical questions signaling genuine evaluation intent, committed to invite additional stakeholders (buyers, accounting, supplier managers) to the 21 July demo, and agreed to share budget/timeline clarity by that date. However, his access to the Economic Buyer is unconfirmed, he has not introduced Zycus to IT or Finance leadership, and he has not advocated against competitors or driven the agenda beyond scheduling the demo. He is engaged and cooperative (active as of 30 June) but has not yet demonstrated advocacy or power access. Champion strength will clarify based on whether he delivers the promised stakeholder attendance and EB intro at or after the demo.

sources: Avoma discovery call 24 June 2026, Cesar Cherlin: 'How does Zycus integrate with SAP?'
Inferred from SAP ERP incumbent stack and enterprise S2P market dynamics
competition.narrative: Competing against SAP Ariba (primary) and Coupa (secondary). Marvin runs SAP ERP (Cesar confirmed 24 June call), giving SAP Ariba a built-in integration advantage and incumbent inertia. Coupa is the market-leading S2P platform and a standard enterprise alternative. Neither competitor has been explicitly named by Cesar, but his question 'How does Zycus integrate with SAP?' signals awareness of SAP Ariba as the natural fit, and enterprise S2P evaluations routinely include Coupa. The wedge is Merlin AI: Zycus differentiates on AI-powered intake, sourcing negotiation agents, and contract intelligence, capabilities neither SAP Ariba nor Coupa match at the same depth. The 21 July demo must lead with Merlin use cases (AI intake, negotiation agent on supplier price history, contract risk scoring) to overcome brand and integration advantages. No competitive review or win/loss analysis has been conducted yet.

sources: Avoma discovery call 24 June 2026, Cesar Cherlin
identify_pain.narrative: Cesar owns the pain. On the 24 June call he stated: 'We're getting dinged on late payments and our buyers are drowning in manual PO creation. We have zero visibility into which suppliers are performing and which are costing us money. Our contract repository is a mess, renewals sneak up on us, and we have no way to track supplier risk.' Pain is operational (manual procurement, late-payment penalties, poor supplier visibility, contract chaos, no risk tracking) and financial (late fees, maverick spend implied), owned by Procurement (Cesar) and impacting Accounting and Supplier Management (both invited to 21 July demo). Pain is confirmed but not yet quantified.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics, legal review process, InfoSec requirements, or procurement channel have been discussed. Marvin runs SAP ERP (confirmed 24 June call), so technical integration validation and IT/InfoSec sign-off will be required, but no stakeholder from those functions has engaged yet. Implementation scoping (professional services, data migration, user training) has not been addressed. economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer identified. Cesar Cherlin (Estimating & Procurement Manager) is the sole contact and is an Evaluator role per Salesforce contact roles. A $150k 7-module S2P investment spanning eProcurement, sourcing, contract management, supplier management, risk, and AI intake will require CFO or COO budget approval, but no power engagement has occurred. The 30 June discovery Next Step committed Cesar to 'share details on priorities, stakeholders, active evaluation, budget, and timeline,' but it is unknown whether he disclosed the approval chain or EB identity. Mapping the EB and securing an introduction is the top commercial-stage blocker.

decision_process.narrative: The buyer is running a structured evaluation: discovery completed 30 June, demo scheduled 21 July with accounting and supplier managers invited, indicating cross-functional review. Cesar stated on 30 June that he will have 'more clarity on timeline by time of Demo,' signaling that the approval process and next gates are not yet locked. No formal RFP stage, vendor shortlist count, or approval chain has been disclosed. IT engagement is pending (Gareth Nicholson, IT Operations Director, has not yet joined calls). The deal is early in Formal Evaluation; the process will clarify post-demo.

sources: Avoma discovery call 24 June 2026, Cesar Cherlin
decision_criteria.narrative: Cesar outlined evaluation priorities on the 24 June call: SAP ERP integration capability, module-by-module implementation flexibility, ease of use for buyers, and supplier adoption. He asked 'Can we buy Zycus module-by-module or does it have to be the full suite?' and 'How does Zycus integrate with SAP?' These questions reveal his criteria (integration fit, implementation flexibility, user adoption) but no formal RFP scorecard, weighted criteria, or test-case validation framework has been shared. The 30 June discovery was scoped to address eProcurement, sourcing, iContract, SRPM, and Merlin Intake, suggesting use-case alignment is in progress. The 21 July demo with accounting and supplier managers will surface additional criteria from cross-functional stakeholders.

flags: Single-threaded risk: only 1 buyer-side contact (Cesar, Estimating & Procurement Manager, Evaluator); no Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, Champion, IT, or Finance engagement yet. The 30 June discovery call is the break-out moment: Cesar committed to inviting 1-2 buyers; push for Robert Wright (Director of Procurement, his manager and the referral source) and a Finance/IT stakeholder (Echo Zhu, Director of Finance; Gareth Nicholson, IT Operations Director) to join, or schedule follow-on calls with them immediately after discovery. A $150k 7-module suite spanning eProcurement, contract lifecycle, AP automation, sourcing, and Merlin AI will require multi-departmental and CFO/COO approval; Cesar alone cannot close this deal.

Economic Buyer and budget authority unmapped: unknown who controls the $150k commercial decision. Cesar to share 'budget' details on 30 June; use that conversation to ask directly: who approves a $150k software investment, what is the approval threshold, and can we meet that person on the next call. Likely candidates: Robert Wright (Director of Procurement), Echo Zhu (Director of Finance), or David Gussman (President & COO). Map and engage the EB within 7 days of the discovery call or the 23 July close date becomes indefensible.

Merlin AI is the wedge vs SAP Ariba and Coupa: Cesar attended a Zycus-sponsored ProCureCon event 'very AI focused' and stated strong appetite for AI automation to 'take the next step moving forward' (Avoma 24 June 2026). SAP Ariba's AI is nascent; Coupa's AI is less advanced than Zycus Merlin (autonomous sourcing/negotiation agent, Merlin Intake natural-language PR creation). Make Merlin the hero of the 30 June discovery demo: show live Merlin Intake PR creation via natural language (solves adoption pain), autonomous negotiation agent (solves sourcing cycle time), and contract AI for renewal tracking and obligation extraction (solves contract-repository pain). Differentiate on innovation, not just functional parity, to overcome the SAP incumbent advantage and Coupa brand strength.

SAP is the incumbent ERP, giving SAP Ariba a built-in integration advantage and incumbent inertia. Coupa is the market-leading S2P alternative. Unknown whether either is actively being evaluated or demoed. If Marvin is running a formal RFP with the relevant stakeholder or Coupa in the mix, Zycus must differentiate on Merlin AI innovation to overcome brand and incumbent strength.

Single-threaded risk: only 1 buyer-side contact (Cesar, Estimating & Procurement Manager, Evaluator); no Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, Champion, IT, or Finance engagement yet. The 30 June discovery call is the break-out moment: Cesar committed to inviting 1-2 buyers; push for Robert Wright (Director of Procurement, his manager and the referral source) and a Finance/IT stakeholder (Echo Zhu, Director of Finance; Gareth Nicholson, IT Operations Director) to join, or schedule follow-on calls with them immediately after discovery. A $150k 7-module suite spanning eProcurement, contract lifecycle, AP automation, sourcing, and Merlin AI will require multi-departmental and CFO/COO approval; Cesar alone cannot close this deal.

Economic Buyer and budget authority unmapped: unknown who controls the $150k commercial decision. Cesar to share 'budget' details on 30 June; use that conversation to ask directly: who approves a $150k software investment, what is the approval threshold, and can we meet that person on the next call. Likely candidates: Robert Wright (Director of Procurement), Echo Zhu (Director of Finance), or David Gussman (President & COO). Map and engage the EB within 7 days of the discovery call or the 23 July close date becomes indefensible.

Merlin AI is the wedge vs SAP Ariba and Coupa: Cesar attended a Zycus-sponsored ProCureCon event 'very AI focused' and stated strong appetite for AI automation to 'take the next step moving forward' (Avoma 24 June 2026). SAP Ariba's AI is nascent; Coupa's AI is less advanced than Zycus Merlin (autonomous sourcing/negotiation agent, Merlin Intake natural-language PR creation). Make Merlin the hero of the 30 June discovery demo: show live Merlin Intake PR creation via natural language (solves adoption pain), autonomous negotiation agent (solves sourcing cycle time), and contract AI for renewal tracking and obligation extraction (solves contract-repository pain). Differentiate on innovation, not just functional parity, to overcome the SAP incumbent advantage and Coupa brand strength.
